metadata.from: Headquarters AAF Overseas Replacement Depot and AAF Redistribution Station Number 5, Greensboro, North Carolina (official military document signed by Captain William F. Shafer, Adjutant, by order of Colonel Younts)
metadata.to: Pvt Robert E. Hanifen, 424 Virginia Avenue, Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.envelope_notes: Official government envelope from War Department, Headquarters Fort Des Moines, Army Post Branch, Des Moines, Iowa. Marked 'OFFICIAL BUSINESS'. Upper right corner shows 'PENALTY FOR PRIVATE PAYMENT OF POSTA[GE]'. The envelope was sent FROM Fort Des Moines TO Pvt Robert E. Hanifen at 424 Virginia Avenue — suggesting Bob had already been discharged or was on furlough at his home address when this was forwarded or mailed directly. The postmark is Des Moines, Dec 15, 1945.
metadata.mood_tone: Formal, bureaucratic military document. No personal emotional tone; purely administrative orders regarding furlough extensions and reassignment.
metadata.legibility_notes: Document is typed and generally legible. Some pages appear to be duplicates or carbon copies. Minor wear and foxing on paper. A few words slightly obscured by folds or age. Pages 2 and 3 appear to be duplicates (carbon copies of the same content), as do pages 1 and 4, and pages 5 and 6.
metadata.historical_context: This is a WWII demobilization-era document from November 1945, shortly after the end of World War II. It is an extract of Special Orders Number 331 from Headquarters AAF Overseas Replacement Depot and AAF Redistribution Station Number 5 in Greensboro, North Carolina. The orders extend furloughs for various enlisted men (including Pvt Robert E. Hanifen) who were granted 30-day reenlistment furloughs effective November 4, 1945. The extensions are granted 'for convn of the govt' (convenience of the government), pushing return dates to January 7-8, 1946. This reflects the massive demobilization effort following V-J Day in August 1945.

pages.transcription:
WAR DEPARTMENT
Headquarters, Fort Des Moines
  Army Post Branch
Des Moines, - Iowa
OFFICIAL BUSINESS

[Postmark: DES MOINES / DEC 15 / 2 PM / 1945 / IOWA]

[Upper right: PENALTY FOR PRIVATE / PAYMENT OF POSTA[GE]]

PVT ROBERT E. HANIFEN
424 VIRGINIA AVENUE
DES MOINES, IOWA.

summary: This is an official U.S. Army War Department document — a restricted extract of Special Orders Number 331, dated 27 November 1945, from Headquarters AAF Overseas Replacement Depot and AAF Redistribution Station Number 5 in Greensboro, North Carolina. The document extends the reenlistment furloughs of numerous enlisted men, including Pvt Robert E. Hanifen (ASN 17170600) of 424 Virginia Avenue, Des Moines, Iowa, by 34 days for the convenience of the government, pushing his required return date to midnight 7 January 1946. The document was mailed from Fort Des Moines and postmarked December 15, 1945, and arrived at Bob Hanifen's home address. Three carbon copies of the two-page order are included in the envelope, all signed by Captain William F. Shafer, Adjutant, by order of Colonel Younts. This document is significant as it captures Bob Hanifen's status in the immediate post-WWII demobilization period, showing him on extended furlough at his Des Moines home address.
